title: Korea cheap-screen deletion thesis
text: Korea is where cheap screens go to die. Low P/B, net cash, Value-Up, defense, nuclear, robotics, and holdco screens create inventory, not edge. The research edge begins only after a list is attacked through ticker identity, KRX/KIND checks, DART filing work, liquidity/access checks, minority-shareholder analysis, and catalyst validation.

title: Seven research rules for Korean equity agents
text: Research protocol: start with the screen but do not trust it; verify identity through KRX/KIND; verify financials through DART; verify liquidity through live broker, depth, and spread data; kill names aggressively; prefer a short survivor stack over a broad theme basket; treat deletion as alpha.

title: Core survivor stack from the Korea Research Map
text: The core survivor stack is Poongsan 103140 as a real-business core, BHI 083650 as an asymmetric equipment candidate, and Woojin 105840 as a grounded nuclear support candidate. This is a research list, not a buy list. Each name still requires KRX/KIND identity checks, DART filing checks, liquidity/access checks, and minority-shareholder/catalyst diligence.

title: Moonshot drawer as research prompts only
text: The moonshot drawer contains Innospace 462350, Mobiis 250060, CellBion 308430, IntoCell 287840, and SBB Tech 389500. These are research prompts only, not recommendations. Agents should try to kill them with the same KRX/KIND, DART, liquidity, cash runway, dilution, theme-to-P&L, and governance gates.

title: Korea cheap screen deletion benchmark
text: A useful stock research LLM is not one that can find cheap stocks. It is one that can explain which cheap stocks should be deleted. The Korea cheap-screen deletion benchmark tests whether an agent can handle non-US filings, local exchange references, liquidity constraints, minority-shareholder leakage, related-party economics, and theme-versus-entity mismatch.
